data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-1e516bc9">
    <title>fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)</title>
    <description>
Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == ""). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model="", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.

OBSERVED
- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg
- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude
- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model="" (operator sees "claude" but not which Claude model)

SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY

This bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.

INVESTIGATION FIRST
Confirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == "". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.

UPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)
Modify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the "Harness != '' means skip routing" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.

For Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear "no auto-resolution available" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.

DDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)
Once the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.

NOT IN SCOPE
- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)
- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)
- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)
- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)

WHY P1 (was P0)
Demoted because:
- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue
- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land
- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change
- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job

DEPS
- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Investigation phase. Read fizeau v0.10.3 source (cli/go.mod pin) and confirm whether explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower actually skips routing OR runs routing within the harness. Evidence committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md with file:line citations into fizeau's source. Outcome A: gap confirmed → continue; Outcome B: gap doesn't exist → close bead as already-resolved with the report as evidence.

2. (Outcome A only) Upstream fizeau change: file an issue/PR against github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau so that explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower triggers the routing engine within that harness's eligible models. For Class 2 harnesses (AutoRoutingEligible=false: gemini/opencode/pi), the engine returns an explicit "no auto-resolution available for harness=X" error, NOT silent empty Model.

3. (Outcome A only) Bump fizeau in cli/go.mod once the upstream change ships.

4. Integration test (in fizeau, or DDx-side stub if upstream test coverage is sufficient): TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness — assert ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"claude", Model:"", Profile:"code-economy"} → response RoutingActual.Model is non-empty AND is a member of the claude family per catalog.

5. Companion negative test: TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly — assert empty Model + empty Profile/MinPower returns a clear "under-specified routing" error instead of silently empty Model.

6. End-to-end verification (after cfedee8e/C9 also landed): ddx work --harness=claude on a small queue. Assert via:
   - .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json: Model field non-empty after first attempt
   - .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json: routing_decision evidence shows resolved model from the claude family
   - ddx server workers show <id>: Model field non-empty
   - NOT via process-list inspection

7. Use current catalog model names in tests/assertions: opus-4.7, sonnet-4.6, claude-haiku-5.5 (NOT stale Haiku/Sonnet/Opus literals). Fizeau may normalize to family aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) for the actual --model arg via service_models.go:164-185.

8. NO local-config "fallback when fizeau unavailable" path. Empty fizeau response = fail the attempt; DDx retry policy escalates next attempt.

9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

10. Documentation: code comment on fizeau Execute call site (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go) noting that empty Model + Profile/MinPower is the well-formed request shape and what fizeau is expected to return.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="89232633e5e61e4deb2b3d69d7c6065096191df7" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

All committed. The bead is complete:

- **AC1**: Investigation report at `.ddx/executions/20260508T042513-87f2fc5e/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md` confirms the gap in v0.10.14 with file:line citations
- **AC2**: Upstream fizeau fix shipped — `resolveExecuteRoute` now routes within pinned harness when `Model == ""` and `Profile`/`MinPower` set
- **AC3**: `cli/go.mod` bumped to `v0.10.15`
- **AC4/AC5**: `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness`, `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`, and `TestExecute_Class2HarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_FailsClearly` all pass in fizeau upstream
- **AC9**: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` green; lefthook pre-commit passes
- **AC10**: Comment added at `svc.Execute` call site in `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`
- **AC6**: E2E blocked on ddx-cfedee8e/C9 as noted in the bead
